narrative: Dear Alex, imagine the admissions team looking at your application like piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns in thousands of similar paths. Your Year 3 decile of 4 stood out as the biggest chapter pulling toward a tougher road ahead—it's the strongest signal in your profile, suggesting challenges in sustaining strong performance as law school demands ramped up. Building on that, your Year 1 decile of 7 reinforced this narrative, showing an early dip that the model sees as a key predictor of later struggles. Even with your upper-middle family income, which might offer some support, it actually tipped the scales toward higher risk in the eyes of past outcomes the team considered. Your LSAT score of 31 and undergraduate GPA of 2.7 added to this picture, painting you as someone who might need extra hurdles cleared to hit that passing mark. Your white background and full-time study plans didn't shift the story much in a positive way here, though your gender was one of the few bright spots, gently nudging toward success. All together, these factors wove a tale where the model estimated a 46% chance of bar failure—enough to pause and say, "Not this round." It's not the end of your story, though; many have rewritten theirs by targeting those early deciles with focused prep. You've got solid foundations to build on—what's your next chapter?
